---
title: "Realistic Evaluation"
description: "Book overview and study-note index for Realistic Evaluation."
book_id: "realistic-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Book"
citation_key: "pawson_tilley_1997_realistic_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:introduction:c0011"
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:ch03-chapter-3-in-with-the-new-introducing-scientific-realism:c0051"
  - "chunk:realistic-evaluation:ch09-chapter-9-the-new-rules-of-realistic-evaluation:c0008"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
<div class="landing-stack">

::: {.landing-hero}
<span class="hero-eyebrow">Book notes</span>

# Realistic Evaluation

Study notes on *Realistic Evaluation* [@pawson_tilley_1997_realistic_evaluation]. These notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

<div class="page-metadata">
  <span>3 major-section notes</span>
  <span>1997</span>
  <span>Source status: needs_review</span>
  <span>MCP evaluation-texts</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.section-grid}
::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>

### [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these notes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-map">Map</span>

### [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)

Selected source sections, page ranges, and reading routes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<span class="page-badge badge-practice">Practice</span>

### [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the notes.
:::
:::

</div>

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>1997</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Book overview</span></div>

This index summarises generated study notes for Ray Pawson and Nick Tilley's *Realistic Evaluation* [@pawson_tilley_1997_realistic_evaluation]. The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

## Core Argument

Realist evaluation studies programs through context-mechanism-outcome configurations rather than asking only whether a whole program worked.

## Major-Section Notes

- [Reader's Guide to Context, Mechanism, and Outcome](section-01-reader-guide-cmo.qmd) - source pages 14-15
- [Scientific Realism and CMO Hypotheses](section-02-scientific-realism-and-cmo.qmd) - source pages 94-95
- [The New Rules and CMO Configurations](section-03-new-rules-cmo-configurations.qmd) - source pages 234

## References
